title: parse_sse_block returns SseEvent::Unknown (not Err) for keepalives and unrecognized event types
description: parse_sse_block() returns (SseEvent::Unknown, None) for empty blocks, keepalives, unknown event types, and malformed JSON payloads — it never returns an error. A future contributor might refactor this to return Result<SseEvent, ParseError> to make failures explicit. That would be wrong: RFC 8895 requires clients to silently ignore unknown event types and comment lines. Keepalives (empty blocks) are mandatory in SSE to keep connections alive through load balancers. If Unknown were an error, the subscribe_events() stream loop would terminate on the first keepalive. The Unknown variant in a non_exhaustive enum forces callers to handle the case without requiring them to understand it.
close_reason: Decision record — current design is correct, no change needed
